scope: Codex right-panel Browser plugin, mobile/iPhone-style visual pass. Guest mode was already active; the Continue without signing in screen did not appear in this browser state.

actions.label: Continue without signing in button not present because guest mode already active

findings.title: PRO modal lacks an obvious close control
findings.detail: The paywall sheet has payment CTAs and a drag handle, but no visible close button; tapping the expected top-right close area did not dismiss it. Backdrop tap worked, but that affordance is hidden.
findings.recommendation: Add a visible, labeled close button in the PRO modal header and keep it above payment CTAs.

findings.title: Header icon buttons and FAB are unlabeled
findings.detail: The accessibility snapshot exposes search, notifications, FAB, and some modal close controls as unnamed buttons. This hurts screen-reader use and makes automated QA brittle.
findings.recommendation: Add aria-labels such as Search, Notifications, Add subscription, and Close to icon-only buttons.

findings.title: Next.js dev issue badge blocks mobile navigation during local QA
findings.detail: The red Next.js issue badge overlaps the lower-left tab area and initially prevented the Analytics tab from activating until the badge was minimized.
findings.recommendation: Fix the underlying dev warning and, for mobile QA, account for the dev overlay or run production preview.

findings.title: Console shows hydration mismatch and Telegram launch-params errors outside Telegram
findings.detail: The app logs a React hydration mismatch on html style attributes and an unhandled Telegram analytics launch-params error when opened in a normal browser.
findings.recommendation: Move Telegram-only analytics behind an environment check and make server/client HTML attributes deterministic.

findings.title: Empty Analytics and Calendar states do not provide an inline recovery action
findings.detail: Both screens explain that subscriptions are needed, but the empty-state area itself has no Add subscription CTA; users must infer the bottom FAB.
findings.recommendation: Add a small inline Add subscription button or link in empty states.

findings.title: Add subscription form has a long scroll to the primary submit action
findings.detail: The required fields are clear after animation settles, but the submit button is far below the first viewport and not sticky.
findings.recommendation: Consider a sticky footer submit action or a shorter first-pass form with advanced fields collapsed by default.

findings.title: Notification enabled state lacks permission/status clarity
findings.detail: Settings showed Enable notifications as active, but the browser context also logged Telegram/haptic environment warnings and no clear permission status was visible.
findings.recommendation: Show browser/Telegram permission status near the toggle, especially outside Telegram.
